title: Trade Finance Operations Lead, Vice President
description:
Location: Singapore
Reporting To: Managing Director, Regional Head of Trade Finance Operations
Industry: Banking / Financial Services
Function: Operations – Trade Finance
Type: Full-Time, 100% OnSite (Office), Leadership Role

Overview

We are conducting a confidential executive search for a dynamic and forward-thinking Lead Role with potential career progression to lead and transform our Trade Finance Operations department. This strategic leadership role is pivotal in shaping the future of our trade finance capabilities, ensuring operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and innovation in a rapidly evolving financial landscape.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership & Transformation

  • Champion a transformational agenda for Trade Finance Operations, aligning operational capabilities with the bank’s strategic direction and future growth.

  • Lead the reimagining of trade finance processes, leveraging automation, digital platforms, and data analytics to drive efficiency, scalability, and customer-centricity.

  • Identify and implement innovative solutions to streamline workflows, reduce manual touchpoints, and enhance turnaround times across trade products.

  • Foster a culture of continuous improvement, encouraging experimentation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration to solve complex operational challenges.

Operational Excellence & Delivery

  • Oversee the full spectrum of trade finance operations including Letters of Credit, Documentary Collections, Bank Guarantees, and Supply Chain Finance.

  • Ensure robust controls and governance are in place to maintain high levels of accuracy, timeliness, and compliance in transaction processing.

  • Drive performance metrics and KPIs to monitor operational health, proactively address bottlenecks, and deliver consistent service excellence.

Risk & Regulatory Management

  • Maintain a strong risk management framework, ensuring adherence to internal policies and external regulatory requirements (e.g., MAS, ICC, AML/CFT).

  • Lead the department’s readiness for regulatory audits and inspections, ensuring transparency, traceability, and responsiveness.

  • Anticipate emerging risks and regulatory changes, and proactively adapt operational strategies to mitigate impact.

Stakeholder Engagement & Influence

  • Act as a key liaison between Trade Finance Operations and stakeholders including Business Units, Compliance, Risk, Technology, and external partners.

  • Influence senior leadership and cross-functional teams by providing strategic insights and operational intelligence to support decision-making.

  • Collaborate with product and technology teams to co-create digitally enabled trade solutions that enhance client experience and operational agility.

People Leadership & Culture Building

  • Lead, mentor, and inspire a diverse team of trade finance professionals, cultivating a high-performance and resilient workforce.

  • Drive talent development and succession planning, ensuring the team is future-ready and equipped with the skills needed for transformation.

  • Promote a culture of accountability, adaptability, and innovation, empowering teams to take ownership and deliver impactful results.

Key Requirements

  • Proven experience in Trade Finance Operations, with deep domain expertise across products and processes.

  • Demonstrated success in leading transformational initiatives and driving operational change.

  • Strong understanding of risk management, regulatory frameworks, and compliance in trade finance.

  • Excellent stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across functions.

  • Resilient, results-driven, and able to perform under pressure in a fast-paced environment.

  • Strategic thinker with a hands-on approach and a passion for operational excellence.

  • Prior leadership experience in a banking or financial services environment is essential.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, or related field.

  • Professional certifications in Trade Finance or Risk Management are advantageous.

  • Experience with digital transformation, automation, process optimization and emerging technologies in trade finance operations.

  • Minimally 15 years’ hands on experience in relevant experience.

postedCompany.description:
The longest-established Singapore bank, we’ve been helping people and businesses get what they want from life since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand their needs. And it starts with looking after our people. With a motivated, purpose-driven culture. Looking after your well-being. Providing continuous holistic growth. 


Today, we are on a journey of transformation. Pioneering banking solutions with a future-ready mindset. Unleashing the creativity and ambition of our people though the largest-of-its-kind open-learning program. Harnessing the power of seamless data and technology to create first-to-market products and services. Partnering with sustainability innovators. Financing green technologies. Working in close-knit collaborative teams. Where you feel included and in complete control of your career growth. Now and into the future. 


We are recognised as one of the World's Top 50 Safest Banks by Global Finance. And the best-managed bank in Singapore and Asia Pacific by The Asian Banker. From this foundation of financial strength and robust technology, we serve 3.5 million people across Asia. Our footprint covers over 1,000 branches throughout Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, China, Hong Kong and Macao.  


Now it's time to launch your career at the forefront of banking. Whether you want to innovate needs-based financial services. Find the best version of yourself in a friendly, supportive team. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career, your Opportunity Starts Here.
postedCompany.name: OVERSEA-CHINESE BANKING CORPORATION LIMITED
